title: saprosma foetens subsp. ceylanica
intro: Saprosma foetens subsp. ceylanica, also known as the Ceylonese Saprosma, is a plant species native to Sri Lanka, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Saprosma foetens subsp. ceylanica include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by drying and powdering the leaves or roots, which can then be consumed as a decoction or used topically as a paste or ointment.
